steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively emphasizes how long Akari Narita competed. To show duration, a choice must explicitly name both the start year (1996) and end year (2004) of her career — not just medals won or debut year alone.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — During her career as a Paralympic swimmer, Akari Narita took home a total of twenty medals, fifteen of which were gold.: emphasizes medal count, not the length of her career. ✗
  • Choice B — Swimming, a sport in which Akari Narita took home fifteen gold medals, has been an event at the Paralympic Games since 1960.: focuses on the sport's history and her gold medals, not her personal career span. ✗
  • Choice C — An accomplished Paralympian with multiple gold medal wins, Akari Narita competed as a swimmer from 1996 to 2004.: explicitly states both the start (1996) and end (2004) of her career, directly emphasizing its duration. ✓
  • Choice D — Cross-country skier Hans Höfle made his Paralympic debut in 1988, while swimmer Akari Narita made her debut in 1996.: shows only Narita's debut year and shifts focus to Höfle, not her career length. ✗
That matches Choice C — An accomplished Paralympian with multiple gold medal wins, Akari Narita competed as a swimmer from 1996 to 2004..
